data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-aede917d">
    <title>ddx work: drain pause/resume on NoViableProviderForNow + bead-claim release</title>
    <description>
Upstream Fizeau bead agent-63bf58a6 adds a NoViableProviderForNow typed error returned when all providers are quota-exhausted. DDx must consume it correctly:

1. When the drain loop receives NoViableProviderForNow, do NOT mark the bead as failed
2. Release the bead's claim so other workers (different machines, different provider config) can pick it up
3. Sleep until error.retry_after (with bounded max — default 30 min, configurable via --quota-pause-max)
4. On wake: probe by attempting the next ready bead. If still NoViableProviderForNow, sleep again with backoff
5. If retry_after is far in the future (> --quota-pause-max), exit the worker with a clear 'quota exhausted, expected recovery <timestamp>; relaunch when ready' message — operator decides when to relaunch
6. Per-worker emit a structured event ('drain-paused-quota') visible in ddx server workers list so operators can see paused workers

This bead is downstream of Fizeau agent-63bf58a6 — depends on its tagged release. Until that lands, DDx falls back to today's behavior (failing beads on quota errors).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Drain loop handles NoViableProviderForNow distinct from execution_failed per TD-NNN QuotaPauseContract (per ddx-673833f4). 2. **Quota pause is worker-state per QuotaPauseContract — bead status is NOT changed (claim is released; status stays 'open' or 'in_progress' per existing 6-value enum). bd/br compatibility (ADR-004) preserved.** 3. drain-paused-quota event emitted with canonical name per TD's event vocabulary. 4. Bead claim released on quota-pause (sibling worker pickup test verifies). 5. Sleep + probe loop honors retry_after; --quota-pause-max bounds total wait. 6. drain-paused-quota event visible in ddx server workers list. 7. New test: spawn 2 workers, simulate one provider quota-exhausting, assert claim released + sibling picks up. 8. Worker exits cleanly with operator-actionable message when retry_after exceeds quota-pause-max. 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:10, area:agent, kind:feature, blocked-on-upstream:agent-63bf58a6</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="51b67917890873325f947a03f07759749206c334" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260502T192934-7a9b3770"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260502T192934-7a9b3770/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260502T192934-7a9b3770/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260502T192934-7a9b3770/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260502T192934-7a9b3770/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output: No commit is being made. Bead `ddx-aede917d` is `blocked-on-upstream:agent-63bf58a6`; Fizeau v0.10.0 (the pinned release) does not yet export `NoViableProviderForNow`, and project policy forbids a local `replace` directive. Wrote `.ddx/executions/20260502T192934-7a9b3770/no_changes_rationale.txt` documenting the block and the follow-up plan once Fizeau ships a tag with the typed error.
